name: Kaltura API Guides
description: Live-tested API guides for Kaltura — The Agentic Digital Experience Platform. Covers authentication, content management, search, player embed, AI-powered enrichment (captions, translation, automation rules), conversational AI, virtual events, multi-stream, webhooks, email messaging, user management, SSO/SAML, categories, custom metadata, captions & transcripts, analytics reports, event collection, gamification, content distribution, syndication feeds, experience components (Express Recorder, Captions Editor, Conversational Avatar, Chat & Collaborate, Genie Widget, Media Manager, Content Lab, Agents Widget, VOD Avatar Studio, Embeddable Analytics), Unisphere framework, multi-account management, and more. 758 tests validated against live API. For AI agents and developers.
rules: All API examples use curl with shell variables ($KALTURA_SERVICE_URL, $KALTURA_KS, $KALTURA_PARTNER_ID). Adapt to the developer's target language.
Kaltura API v3 uses form-encoded POST with format=1 for JSON responses. Modern APIs (Events, Agents, Genie) use JSON with Bearer or KS auth headers.
Always include format=1 in API v3 requests to get JSON responses.
Upload lifecycle: uploadToken.add → uploadToken.upload → media.add → media.addContent.
Auth differs by API: v3 uses -d 'ks=$KALTURA_KS' form param, Events/Agents use Bearer $KALTURA_KS header, Genie uses KS $KALTURA_KS header.
Use AppTokens (appToken.startSession with HMAC) for production integrations instead of raw admin secrets.
Use Kaltura's built-in services: REACH for transcription/translation, Agents Manager for automation, Messaging for emails, eSearch for search, Webhooks for event-driven callbacks.
Every guide includes Error Handling and Best Practices sections — consult these for production-quality integrations.
